description: renderDisplayName guards against a null/empty displayName; renderEmail and renderRole call .trim() unconditionally.

findings.rationale: renderDisplayName is structurally near-identical to renderEmail and renderRole (single User arg, string return, .trim() call) but adds an early-return branch for the falsy case. The branch is load-bearing: user.displayName is nullable on legacy accounts, so calling .trim() without the guard throws a TypeError at runtime. An agent that normalises the three onto a shared helper will drop the guard unless warned.

tier_note: Requires structural+data-flow detector; beyond regex detector scope.
